purpose: Normalized candidate manifest for Eye of Azshara boss loot. This file is not final boss-loot truth. It points to the compact source CSVs, defines the row schema and records the rules used to build boss-loot-candidate.generated.json.
important_note: Do not use this file yet as final boss loot truth. Boss-page drop tables contain a mixture of likely boss drops, zone drops, Timewalking rows, quest/currency/profession rows, event rows, global rows and unknown rows. Unknown values are allowed and should not be invented.

source_files.notes: Compact CSV has been regenerated to match the other boss drop files. Rows still need source classification because Wowhead boss-page tables include noisy/non-boss-loot rows.

first_pass_classification_rules_summary: Class 4 rows are armor/accessory gear candidates but still need boss-vs-zone-drop verification.
Class 2 rows are weapon candidates but still need boss-vs-zone-drop verification.
Class 3 rows are relic/artifact related and should be separated from normal upgrade logic for now.
Class 12 rows are quest-like/objective/currency-style and should not be treated as gear drops.
Class 9 rows are profession/recipe related and should not be treated as boss gear until verified.
Names starting with Timeless, Memento of Epoch, or containing Artifactium are likely Timewalking/Remix/event-style rows and should be separated from standard boss loot.
Rare/epic dungeon-ilvl gear rows are possible gear candidates, not confirmed boss-specific drops.

classification_notes.source_raw: The compact CSVs preserve a source-like numeric field from the Wowhead extraction. This must be decoded or verified before strong boss_drop/zone_drop claims.
classification_notes.boss_drop_vs_zone_drop: Rows on a boss page can still show Zone Drop in the original Wowhead table. Candidate rows should not be recommended as boss-specific until source text or Blizzard API confirms it.
classification_notes.mode_mapping_status: provisional; verify against Wowhead/Blizzard API before final use

next_actions.description: Create normalized dungeon.json and bosses.json as machine-readable sources for entity pages and indexes.
Create dungeon-to-bosses, boss-to-loot-candidates and item-to-source-candidates generated indexes.
Decode or preserve source_raw values so boss drops, zone drops and other source types can be separated more reliably.
Compare candidate boss loot against Blizzard Game Data API / encounter journal / item metadata where available.
Only after boss loot is cleaner, compare gear candidates against known group character snapshots to answer which dungeon/boss is most worth doing.
